claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with a few initial questions:

**What is the name of the child (or children) you'd like me to check eligibility for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'd like to check if I'm eligible for my daughter Alex. She's 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So I'm assessing eligibility for **Alex**, your 6-year-old daughter.

To continue, I need to understand your living situation:

**Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex lives with me, but we don't actually live in the UK. We're living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. That's important information.

Before I make a final determination, let me check the specific eligibility rules to ensure I give you accurate advice.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that context. Based on the eligibility information I've reviewed, I can see that a key requirement is that you must **live in the UK** to qualify for Child Benefit (with some exceptions for certain countries and circumstances).

However, since you mentioned you're living abroad, I need to ask:

**Which country are you currently living in with Alex?**

This will help me determine whether you might fall under any of the exceptions that could allow you to claim Child Benefit.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not say exactly which country, but it's not one of those special situations - I'm not a Crown servant or anything like that. Just living abroad normally.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Based on the information you've provided and the eligibility rules I've reviewed:

Since you and Alex live abroad in a country that isn't one of the special situations covered (such as Crown servants or certain specific countries), and the standard requirement for Child Benefit is that you must live in the UK, I have enough information to make a determination.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Outcome: Not Eligible**

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

The main reason is that Child Benefit requires you to live in the UK. While there are some exceptions (for Crown servants or certain specific countries), you've indicated you're living abroad in a standard situation outside of those exceptions.

If your circumstances change in the future—for example, if you move back to the UK—you would be able to apply for Child Benefit at that point. If you have any other questions or if your situation changes, feel free to reach out.

reasonings: Alex does not qualify for Child Benefit because the standard eligibility requirement is that you must live in the UK to claim. You have indicated that you are living abroad with Alex in a country that does not fall under the exceptions (such as being a Crown servant or living in certain specified countries). Without meeting the UK residency requirement, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for your daughter Alex because you do not meet the primary requirement of living in the UK. While there are some exceptions for certain circumstances (Crown servants and specific countries), you have indicated you are living abroad in a normal situation outside of these exceptions.">

Without meeting the UK residency requirement, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex.
